steps.title: Track the whole passage
steps.content: The passage first describes the Federalist Papers as 85 essays by Hamilton, Jay, and Madison, published to support ratification of the Constitution. It then explains that the authorship of most essays is certain, while that of a few essays is uncertain.

steps.title: Avoid narrower purposes
steps.content: A covers only the argument for ratification. C incorrectly suggests some essays were written by two people, and D covers only publication history. The choice that includes both the description of the collection and what is known about authorship best captures the purpose. The answer is B.
